4: 
5: - The follow-up payload is the **same JSON shape** as `case_v1.json` (top-level case object with a `sections` map). Absence of a section or field key means the AI did not re-extract it — it is not a deletion.
6: - `missing_fields` in the follow-up payload is an **AI output annotation for that extraction run only**; it replaces, not accumulates, any previous `missing_fields`.

"# Tweak Runtime Bootstrap: Apply v2 Follow-up Changes on Startup

## Background

The backend currently seeds in-memory state from `case_v1.json` on startup (via `@PostConstruct` in `CaseStore`). The brief says for Phase 2 the v2 follow-up must be POSTed at the start of the live session so the merged case is immediately queryable.

The ask here is to **tweak the initial runtime case data on startup** — i.e., automatically apply the `case_v2_followup_payload.json` changes during bootstrap so the merged case is ready without a manual POST.

---

## Identified Changes (v1 → v2 diff)

| Section | Field | v1 Value | v2 Value | Merge Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `patient` | `weight_kg` | `"78"` | `"76"` | overridden |
| `suspect_drug` | `dose` | `"20 mg"` | `"40 mg"` | overridden |
| `adverse_event` | `event_term` | `"Myalgia"` | `"Rhabdomyolysis"` | overridden |
| `adverse_event` | `outcome` | `"Recovered"` | `"Recovering"` | overridden |
| `adverse_event` | `seriousness` | `"Non-serious"` | `"Serious"` | overridden |
| `adverse_event` | `hospitalization` | *(absent)* | `"Yes - 5 days"` | new |
| All others | — | same value | same value | unchanged |
| `missing_fields` | — | *(absent)* | `["concomitant_medications", "medical_history", "creatine_kinase_level"]` | new top-level array |

---

## Design Decision

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Two valid approaches — please confirm which you prefer:**
>
> **Option A — Bootstrap merged (recommended for Phase 2):** On startup, load `case_v1.json`, then immediately apply `case_v2_followup_payload.json` through `MergeService`. The store is seeded with the annotated v2-merged case (version 2). `GET /cases/PV-2026-0451` returns the already-merged result. This is what the brief means by "tweaking the initial runtime case data on startup" for the live session.
>
> **Option B — Keep v1 as stored, but pre-load v2 file ready to POST:** No change to startup behavior; the v2 JSON is just a resource ready to be submitte

## v1 → v2 Diff Summary

Six fields change, one new field is added, and `missing_fields` goes from absent to a 3-item array:

| Field | v1 | v2 | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `patient.weight_kg` | `78` | `76` | overridden |
| `suspect_drug.dose` | `20 mg` | `40 mg` | overridden |
| `adverse_event.event_term` | `Myalgia` | `Rhabdomyolysis` | overridden |
| `adverse_event.outcome` | `Recovered` | `Recovering` | overridden |
| `adverse_event.seriousness` | `Non-serious` | `Serious` | overridden |
| `adverse_event.hospitalization` | *(absent)* | `Yes - 5 days` | **new** |
| `missing_fields` | *(absent)* | 3 fields | **new** |

---

## Proposed Approach

The plan implements **Option A** (recommended): on startup, `CaseStore.init()` loads **both** `case_v1.json` and `case_v2_followup_payload.json` from the classpath, runs them through the existing `MergeService`, and seeds the store with the already-merged v2 case. This means `GET /cases/PV-2026-0451` returns version 2 with full merge annotations from the moment the service boots — no manual POST required.

### Files to change
1. **[NEW]** `backend/src/main/resources/case_v2_followup_payload.json` — copy payload onto classpath
2. **[MODIFY]** [`CaseStore.java`](file:///d:/icsr_case_review/backend/src/main/java/com/theragenx/icsr/store/CaseStore.java) — inject `MergeService`, apply follow-up in `@PostConstruct`
3. **[MODIFY]** [`application.properties`](file:///d:/icsr_case_review/backend/src/main/resources/application.properties) — add optional `icsr.bootstrap.apply-followup=true` toggle

> **Key design question in the plan:** The `@IMPORTANT` block in the plan asks whether you want **Option A** (pre-merged on boot, version 2 ready instantly) or **Option B** (store still holds v1, POST is manual). Please review and approve before I make any code changes.

"# Tweak Runtime Bootstrap: Apply v2 Follow-up Changes on Startup

## Background

The backend currently seeds in-memory state from `case_v1.json` on startup (via `@PostConstruct` in `CaseStore`). The brief says for Phase 2 the v2 follow-up must be POSTed at the start of the live session so the merged case is immediately queryable.

The ask here is to **tweak the initial runtime case data on startup** — i.e., automatically apply the `case_v2_followup_payload.json` changes during bootstrap so the merged case is ready without a manual POST.

---

## Identified Changes (v1 → v2 diff)

| Section | Field | v1 Value | v2 Value | Merge Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `patient` | `weight_kg` | `"78"` | `"76"` | overridden |
| `suspect_drug` | `dose` | `"20 mg"` | `"40 mg"` | overridden |
| `adverse_event` | `event_term` | `"Myalgia"` | `"Rhabdomyolysis"` | overridden |
| `adverse_event` | `outcome` | `"Recovered"` | `"Recovering"` | overridden |
| `adverse_event` | `seriousness` | `"Non-serious"` | `"Serious"` | overridden |
| `adverse_event` | `hospitalization` | *(absent)* | `"Yes - 5 days"` | new |
| All others | — | same value | same value | unchanged |
| `missing_fields` | — | *(absent)* | `["concomitant_medications", "medical_history", "creatine_kinase_level"]` | new top-level array |

---

## Design Decision

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Two valid approaches — please confirm which you prefer:**
>
> **Option A — Bootstrap merged (recommended for Phase 2):** On startup, load `case_v1.json`, then immediately apply `case_v2_followup_payload.json` through `MergeService`. The store is seeded with the annotated v2-merged case (version 2). `GET /cases/PV-2026-0451` returns the already-merged result. This is what the brief means by "tweaking the initial runtime case data on startup" for the live session.
>
> **Option B — Keep v1 as stored, but pre-load v2 file ready to POST:** No change to startup behavior; the v2 JSON is just a resource ready to be submitte

"# Changelog

## [2026-07-13] — Auto-apply v2 Follow-up on Startup

### Context

The brief requires that before the Phase 2 live session, `case_v2_followup_payload.json`
is POSTed to the backend so the merged case is immediately queryable. Rather than
doing this manually every time the service restarts, the bootstrap logic was extended to
apply the follow-up automatically at startup.

---

### Changes

#### `backend/src/main/resources/case_v2_followup_payload.json` *(new)*

Copied `assignment_phase_1/case_v2_followup_payload.json` onto the Spring Boot classpath
(`src/main/resources/`) so it can be loaded as a `ClassPathResource` during startup —
same pattern already used for `case_v1.json`.

---

#### `backend/src/main/java/com/theragenx/icsr/store/CaseStore.java` *(modified)*

**Before:** `@PostConstruct init()` loaded `case_v1.json` and seeded the in-memory store
with the raw version-1 case. Constructor accepted only `ObjectMapper`.

**After:** Two-phase bootstrap with a feature toggle:

1. **Step 1 (unchanged)** — Load `case_v1.json` → store as version 1.
2. **Step 2 (new, optional)** — If `icsr.bootstrap.apply-followup=true`, load
   `case_v2_followup_payload.json` and run it through `MergeService.merge()`.
   The annotated version-2 merged case replaces the v1 entry in the store.

Constructor now also accepts `MergeService` (injected by Spring). No circular dependency
risk — `MergeService` has no dependency back on `CaseStore`.

A `loadCaseFromClasspath(String resourceName)` private helper was extracted to avoid
duplicating the try/catch + `ClassPathResource` pattern for both files.
